evals.prompt: I built a customer support agent using OpenAI Agents SDK (Python). The agent has a couple of function tools but no MCP, no hosted tools. I want to add a FastAPI server with an SSE streaming endpoint so my React frontend can call it. I don't need session memory right now, just streaming. Show me the minimal code to wire this up.
evals.expected_output: A FastAPI app with a /stream SSE endpoint wired to the agent. Should include: async def endpoint, Runner.run_streamed(), StreamingResponse with text/event-stream. Should NOT include MCP integration, Hosted Tools, or Tracing code — those sections are irrelevant to this task.

assertions.name: has_streaming_response
assertions.description: Code uses StreamingResponse with media_type text/event-stream

assertions.name: uses_async_def
assertions.description: Endpoint is async def, not sync def

assertions.name: no_mcp_code
assertions.description: Output does NOT include MCP integration code (MCPServerStreamableHttp etc) — that's irrelevant to this task

assertions.name: no_hosted_tools_code
assertions.description: Output does NOT include Hosted Tools code (WebSearchTool, FileSearchTool) — that's irrelevant to this task

evals.prompt: I'm building a WhatsApp bot backend with Python. I have an order-taking agent using OpenAI Agents SDK. Different users need separate conversation history, and I'm running multiple server instances so I need Redis-backed session storage. I want FastAPI endpoints with SSE streaming and Redis sessions. How do I wire this up?
evals.expected_output: FastAPI app with SSE streaming + Redis session storage. Should include: create_agent_router or equivalent, RedisSession or openai-agents[redis] install, session_id per request. Should NOT include MCP or Tracing or Hosted Tools code.

assertions.name: mentions_session_id
assertions.description: Response explains session_id usage per request

assertions.name: no_mcp_code
assertions.description: Output does NOT include MCP integration code — irrelevant to this task

assertions.name: no_hosted_tools_code
assertions.description: Output does NOT include Hosted Tools code — irrelevant to this task

evals.prompt: I have a FastAPI agent backend already running with SSE streaming. Now I want to add an MCP filesystem server (npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem) to my agent so it can read project files. How do I wire the MCP server into my existing FastAPI+agents setup?
evals.expected_output: Code showing MCPServerStdio or MCPServerStreamableHttp wired into the agent, with context manager usage, and how to integrate with the FastAPI lifespan. Should include actual MCP integration code.

assertions.name: has_mcp_server
assertions.description: Response includes actual MCP server setup code

assertions.name: mentions_lifespan_or_context_manager
assertions.description: Response explains how to manage MCP server lifecycle

assertions.name: has_filesystem_example
assertions.description: Response includes filesystem MCP server example
